title: Harness requirements for this project — the canonical process as a state machine, the enforcement-surface audit, and the twenty-requirement set
question: What canonical process must a non-bypassable harness encode for this project, which steps are already mechanically enforced versus honor-system, which observed failure modes must it foreclose at which Claude Code enforcement points, and what requirement set follows?
method: Corpus derivation: CLAUDE.md (pipeline 0-7, Completion Sequence 1-13, Experience-Gap Handling, dispatch fit), all 14 enforced conventions read in full via the CLI, the 9 injected mandates and their UserPromptSubmit delivery, the 15-file memory feedback corpus, the 3 live repo hooks + registrations, husky's 5 commit gates, the substrate's 13 invariants and op surface; per-state classification MECHANICAL/PARTIAL/HONOR; failure modes mapped to observable signals and hook points.
findings_summary: The canonical process distills to a single-track per-task state machine — SURFACED → INVESTIGATED → FILED(provenance) → GATE-VALIDATED → EXPLORED → PLANNED → BASELINED → IMPLEMENTED(subagent) → GATED → DEMOED → PROBED → findings-loop(class-scoped) → DOCS-SYNCED → MERGED → CLOSED → HELD(RELEASED → PUBLISHED) — with exactly three legitimate exits to the user: provenance grants, honestly-underdetermined scope/value escalations, HELD actions. Current mechanical enforcement concentrates at exactly two chokepoints: the substrate op write path (AJV + write-time invariant gate + complete-task atom + attestation) and the husky commit boundary (5 gates) — plus three PreToolUse guards. Honor-system holes: direct Edit/Write on substrate JSON (a parallel ungated path making the op gates bypassable), the ENTIRE verify layer (runtime demo, adversarial probe, iterate-to-zero, subagent-claim verification), plan-mode/subagent-primary implementation discipline, the release hold (nothing blocks npm run release:*), the turn-boundary ask gate (no Stop hook exists anywhere — the observed chaos driver is invisible to every mechanism), and the wiring itself (hook registrations live in untracked settings.local.json; the project mandates.jsonl slot is empty). Failure modes with observable signals: turn-boundary relitigation (Stop hook), skipped pipeline steps (PreToolUse Edit/Write + phase state), self-adjudicated experience gaps (filing-payload investigation citation), deferred findings (findings ledger at merge/close), unverified subagent relays (PostToolUse Agent + turn-end sequence shape), scope reduction (lexical nudge). Twenty requirements: R1-R10 enforceable today as new hook scripts over proven in-repo patterns (substrate-edit block; --no-verify/destructive-git, pipe-masking, and release-hold chokepoints; same-turn sentinel fix; filing-completeness checks; project mandates file; subagent-relay nudge); R11-R17 need one new state layer — phase-state file, Stop-hook ask/convergence gates, findings ledger, session driver — so hooks check 'is this action legal in this state'; R18-R20 cover the milestone gate, status cascade, and tracking the wiring. Design principles: lazy-cooperative threat model, no parallel ungated paths, preserve the three stops, deterministic predicates block while lexical heuristics nudge, filing-pipeline-first before new hooks.
stale_conditions: Any hook added to or removed from .claude/hooks/ or its registrations
The conventions block or injected mandates change materially

citations.label: canonical pipeline + completion sequence + gap handling
findings document: state machine, enforcement classification table, failure-mode signal map, R1-R20
